theme: a genome that develops right despite typos
title: Typo-Proof Genome: Growing a Body Through a Noisy Tape
strategies: do-nothing baseline: a single NOP byte; every cell defaults to type 0 (matches the evaluator's own normalization anchor, ~0.1)

dense relative-chain greedy: CKPT(0) once, then walk left to right with (SET, MOVE(+1)) pairs -- a short, natural exact reconstruction (not the shortest possible; DIV-compression beats it), but every MOVE is load-bearing so one corrupted MOVE permanently shifts the head and cascades wrongness through the rest of the tape

checkpoint-anchored, majority-voted strong: address every cell ABSOLUTELY via CKPT (killing cascades), run-length-compress same-type runs with DIV to free up length budget, then spend the freed budget casting up to 3 independent CKPT+SET votes per cell round-robin so the vote-tally rule majority-corrects a single-bit typo in any one repeat
budget-aware prioritization: with a tight L_max, spread redundancy round-robin across cells instead of front-loading a few, raising the worst cell's vote count first
